summary: Specific terminology (e.g., 'Action Point' or 'Bonus Action') rather than the generic 'Resources' label.
game_context: The player should identify that their 'Action' point for the turn has been depleted.
evidence: UI notification displays 'Not enough resources' in red text when the player attempts to initiate an attack.
uncertain tone
questioning
Scene: A combat encounter where the player is attempting to attack a Goblin Tracker.
Quote: Resources? What? What does that mean?

summary: Clearer distinction that customization for these features is preset-based rather than slider-based.
game_context: The player should select from the provided binary/fixed presets for body parts.
evidence: Navigating between the 'Identity' and 'Genitals' menu options.
questioning
Scene: Appearance tab in the character creator menu during genital customization.
Quote: How do I change my cock size?

summary: The biological nature of the console doesn't immediately suggest its function as a control switch without prior RPG experience.
game_context: Interact with the console to trigger a ship event (releasing or killing a captive).
evidence: Standing stationary in front of the console
Interaction prompt 'A Use' is highlighted
Uncertain, questioning tone
Scene: Nautiloid ship interior, standing in front of a glowing, pulsating biological console labeled 'Neural Apparatus'.
Quote: what is this

summary: None; the UI correctly signaled the damage penalty, but the player was unfamiliar with the 'safe' limits of movement.
game_context: Identify that jumping from a significant height or into hazards results in fall/fire damage.
evidence: Aiming the jump arc across a large distance
Red arc and HP loss notification (-2 HP) visible on the UI
Questioning tone followed by realization
Scene: On the upper levels of the ship, attempting to jump across a gap while environmental fire is nearby.
Quote: Can I? Can I jump all the way over here? Oh I'll lose HP.

summary: A clearer visual transition or 'snap' to available ranged options when a melee path is blocked by resource limits.
game_context: Identify the movement limit and either switch to a ranged attack or end the turn.
evidence: Red 'Not enough movement' pathing line
Repeatedly clicking on an enemy that is clearly highlighted as out of range
Frustrated whispering
Scene: Final combat encounter on the Nautiloid deck against Lesser Imps.
Quote: Not enough movement... kill it... almost... almost...

summary: Visible combat dice (log or floaties) that help bridge the gap between visual proximity and abstract math for new players.
game_context: Understand that visually being 'at' the enemy still requires a successful D20 roll to hit.
evidence: Character is visually touching the enemy
UI displays 'Miss!' after an attack attempt
Disbelieving tone
Defensive tone
Scene: Combat encounter with a Goblin Tracker near the Druid Grove entrance.
Quote: I was right there!
Quote: I didn't roll... I didn't roll.

summary: Condition tooltip or prompt explaining that standing up is tied to the movement resource.
game_context: Wait for the turn to reset and use movement to stand up.
evidence: Character model falls flat
'Prone' condition icon and text appears above the head
Direct question to teammates
Scene: Exploring the dirt path leading to the Chapel entrance, character walks through grease.
Quote: How do I get up?

summary: More prominent highlighting of the 'Action' icon on the hotbar when the resource error occurs.
game_context: Identify that the 'Action' resource has been depleted for the turn.
evidence: UI displays red text 'Not enough resources: Action'
Attack animation does not trigger
Defensive tone
Argumentative
Scene: Combat encounter with looters in the Refectory.
Quote: Dude, I hit him.
Quote: No you didn't.

summary: Better visual feedback or a slower 'activation sequence' for complex environmental traps to allow players to understand the cause-and-effect.
game_context: Observe the environmental triggers (vents, grease) before interacting with the trapped sarcophagus.
evidence: Screen covered in fire and grease explosions
Multiple party members killed instantly
Shocked tone
Genuine confusion
Scene: The Dank Crypt central chamber after interacting with the sarcophagus.
Quote: How'd that happen?
Quote: Did I step on something?
Quote: I don't know what triggered it.

summary: Inventory status update or notification that gear must be manually re-equipped after a full death.
game_context: Recognize that their equipment was left in the inventory/corpse or was removed during the 'downed' state.
evidence: Player character model is naked
Party members looking at the character
Disbelief
Stunned laughter
Scene: Inside the Dank Crypt immediately after the player is resurrected.
Quote: WHAT.
Quote: Why...
Quote: I'm like dead I think.

summary: Visual priority or 'snap-to' highlighting that distinguishes between ally and enemy when their selection hitboxes overlap in tight spaces.
game_context: Target the enemy 'Entombed Warrior' positioned directly behind the teammate.
evidence: Attack animation triggers near teammate
Damage numbers appear on the ally 'Olga Bolshevik'
Uncertain tone
Direct questioning of teammate
Scene: Turn-based combat with skeletons in the Dank Crypt. Multiple player characters and enemies are crowded in a narrow doorway.
Quote: Did I hit the thing?
Quote: Did I hit the thing though?

summary: Clearer floor-plane pathing that accounts for character model collision preventing interactions at 'zero distance'.
game_context: Interact with the corpse to loot it.
evidence: Red pop-up text 'Not enough movement'
Red pop-up text 'Can't reach this'
Assertions of hit/action completion
Argumentative tone
Scene: Post-combat in the Dank Crypt. The player is standing directly over a defeated 'Entombed Warrior' corpse.
Quote: I hit it. I hit the thing.
